mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand and economic growth, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: In months with RSAFS month-over-month increase, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2.75% from a lower level reverses the previous easing stance, tightening financial conditions and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After a FEDFUNDS close above 2.75% following a period below, SPX 20-day returns are negative in less than 60% of occurrences over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm a broad-based economic contraction that depresses corporate earnings and investor sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO decline month-over-month, SPX 60-day returns are negative less than 60% of the time over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: During a high-inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth above 3%), a decline in the federal funds rate signals the Fed is falling behind the curve, exacerbating inflation expectations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During high-inflation episodes (CPI YoY > 3%), the Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and SPX 20-day returns is not negative (i.e., r > -0.1) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong aggregate demand and economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of observations over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market weakness and rising recession risk, which reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE first exceeds 5.5%, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in less than 60% of such instances over any 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing recession (INDPRO year-over-year growth negative for at least three consecutive months), further month-over-month declines in industrial production amplify recession fears and depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During manufacturing recession episodes, the correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and SPX 20-day returns is not significantly negative (Spearman r > -0.1) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakness in both consumer spending and residential investment, reinforcing recession expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month, SPX 60-day returns are negative less than 60% of the time over any 3-year evaluation window.
